english: spelling of ycód-pa (gcod pa) and the assonant verbs spyód-pa (spyod pa) and dpyód-pa (dpyod pa) is variable.

pride, haughtiness, arrogance, bskyúṅ-ba (bskyung ba) to put it off, give it up Lexx.; bčom čuṅ-ṅus (bcom chung ngus) Tar. 20, 6 despondingly, low-spirited; gros-yčóm (gros gcom) Lex. obs. or prov. for gros-bčám (gros bcam), v. °čám-pa (°cam pa).
to spread, scatter, disperse Cs.
v. yčóg-pa (gcog pa) and °čág-pa (°cag pa).
Sch.: bčaṅ-rgya-čén-po (bcang rgya chen po) comprising much, comprehensive, very extensive; bčaṅ-rgyár mdzad-pa (bcang rgyar mdzad pa) resp. to apply one's self, to bestow pains upon'.
W. a whole that has been cut into, or a piece cut off.
W. something old, torn, worn out.
v. °čab-pa (°cab pa).
Sch.: trivial things, medley, hodge-podge.
v. sga (sga).
1. v. čá-ba (ca ba). -- 2. sbst. drinking; gen. used connected with bza-ba (bza ba); bčá-ba (bca' ba) daṅ (dang) bzá-ba (bza' ba), or bza-bča (bza bca) food and drink.
Mil., declivity, precipice Sch.
the usual pronunciation of lčags-mag (lcags mag).

1. = bčir-ba (bcir ba) to squeeze, to press in a press Thgy.; to crowd, to throng, *yár-la bčar* (*yar la bcar*) C., stand (or sit) more closely together! -- 2. to pull or force from, to wrest Cs. -- 3. Lexx.: mig bčár-ba (mig bcar ba) the same as in yčer-mig (gcer mig) (?). -- 4. Sch.: logs bčár-ba (logs bcar ba) to prop sideways. -- 5. Sch.: bčar bzúgs-pa (bcar bzugs pa) to have a permanent residence (this would however be more correctly expressed by čar (car)). -- 6. bčár-bai rta- bčibs (bcar ba'i rta bcibs), and lan-bčár? (lan bcar?) Lexx. w.e.
v. °jál-ba (°jal ba).

1. originally pf. of °čá-ba (°ca ba), little used. -- 2. adj. together with, connected with, having, possessing, containing a thing, with daṅ (dang) or termin. (the latter in prose only when a second daṅ (dang), signifying 'and', occurs in the sentence); gerundially: bčás-te (bcas te), sometimes also bčás-pas (bcas pas) or bčás-śiṅ (bcas shing); adverbially: bčás-su (bcas su) frq.; °Kór daṅ bčás-pa (°khor dang bcas pa)(-te, -su) with attendance, with a retinue or suite, frq.; bu-mo bču bod-blón daṅ bčás-pas skór-te (bu mo bcu bod blon dang bcas pas skor te) surrounded by ten virgins together with the Tibetan ambassadors Glr.; btsún-mo daṅ srás-su bčás-te (btsun mo dang sras su bcas te) with (his) wife and son Glr.; gos daṅ bčás-su (gos dang bcas su) (to go into the water) having one's clothes on Dzl.; żal °dzúm-pa daṅ bčás-te (zhal °dzum pa dang bcas te) with a smiling face Glr.; sér-sna daṅ bčás-pa (ser sna dang bcas pa) infected with, subject to, avarice; without daṅ (dang) or termin. (esp. po.): °khrúl-bčás (°khrul bcas) infatuated, fascinated Pth.; °bru-táṅ tuṅ bčás (°bru tang tung bcas) together with a small parcel of Dutan tea; it is also, like rnams (rnams), a collective sign, used in enumerations, referring to several nouns, Wdṅ., or like la-sógs-pa (la sogs pa) and other (things), and more (such things), and the like: rgyags daṅ bčás bskyál-lo (rgyags dang bcas bskyal lo) provisions and other necessaries we shall supply Mil.
fut. of °číṅ-ba (°cing ba) to bind.
pf. of °číṅ-ba (°cing ba) to bind. Both verbs (bčíṅ-ba (bcing ba) and bčíṅs-pa (bcings pa)) are also used as substantives: bonds, fetters, whether of a material, spiritual, or magical nature.
v. °číb-pa (°cib pa); Sch. also: carriage, conveyance.
v. °čír-ba (°cir ba).
v. °jíl-ba (°jil ba).

(Bal. *wču* (*wcu*)) ten, bču tam-pa (bcu tam pa) id.; bču-pŕag (bcu phrag) a decade; bču-yčíg (bcu gcig), bču-yñís (bcu gnyis) (Bal. *wču - ṅas* (*wcu ngas*)) eleven, twelve etc., (v. also bčo (bco)); bčú-pa (bcu pa), bčú-po (bcu po) as in dgú-pa (dgu pa), dgú-po (dgu po). -- bču-skór °ton (bcu skor °ton), bčù-gyúr °ton (bcu gyur °ton) (the field) yields a tenfold crop. -- *čú-ka* (*cu ka*), *čú-kai ṭal* (*cu ka'i ṭal*) C., *ču-kág* (*cu kag*) W., tithe, tithes; bču-kág-pa (bcu kag pa) a collector of tithes, bču-kag °dón-pa (bcu kag °don pa) to tithe, to decimate Cs. -- bču-dpón (bcu dpon) corporal, Lat. decurio, bču-°óg (bcu °og) (*ču- (*cu-)
